chief_complaint: A man in his mid-70s presented with recent-onset angina.
history: The patient was started on treatment under the diagnosis of acute coronary syndrome. He developed haematemesis and dysphagia 12 hours after admission.
exam: No specific physical examination findings mentioned.
labs: ECG revealed ST-segment depression. Echocardiography showed a circular mass posterior to the left atrium. CT revealed oesophageal submucosal haematoma.
